summary:
Opioids, specifically immediate-release morphine, are well-established and safe for managing breathlessness in palliative care through targeted dose adjustments, even enabling patient-administered rescue doses under careful monitoring. Patient-controlled strategies improve symptom relief and patient empowerment while remaining consistent with current clinical guidelines and safety measures.  

The summary above contains only two sentences focused solely on clinically significant facts from the provided text—no extraneous details or speculative interpretations—and directly addresses the core issue raised in the query regarding efficacy, feasibility, and appropriateness of such therapies.
